# Implementation Plan: SVG Shape Engine v0.1

**Branch**: `001-svg-shape-engine` | **Date**: 2026-03-01 | **Spec**: [spec.md](./spec.md)
**Input**: Feature specification from `/specs/001-svg-shape-engine/spec.md`

**Note**: This plan was generated by the `/speckit.plan` command.

## Summary

Pure TypeScript library with a single `generate()` entry point that accepts a seed, canvas dimensions, an `outputMode`, and an array of typed shape descriptors; returns a deterministic SVG string plus shape-count metadata. Semantic output (`<rect>`, `<circle>`, `<polygon>`) is the default; an optional `"path"` mode renders every shape as a `<path>` element. Shapes are rendered in input order; IDs are derived from seed, type, and index with no runtime randomness.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: TypeScript 5.4 (strict mode)
**Primary Dependencies**: None at runtime; Vitest (dev)
**Storage**: N/A
**Testing**: Vitest 1.x
**Target Platform**: Node.js / any ES2020-capable JS runtime (library)
**Project Type**: Library
**Performance Goals**: Byte-for-byte deterministic output; no measurable per-call overhead at typical design-tool scales
**Constraints**: Zero runtime dependencies; pure function; no side effects; no I/O
**Scale/Scope**: Single entry point; 4 primitive shape types; two output modes

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.*

| Principle | Status | Notes |
|-----------|--------|-------|
| Scope Discipline | ✅ PASS | Only geometric shape generation. No composition, layout, color theory, or text placement. |
| Composability | ✅ PASS | Each shape is a self-contained SVG element with no assumption about context. |
| Incremental Expansion | ✅ PASS | Stage 1 only. `outputMode` is additive; core `generate()` signature unchanged. |
| Deterministic Variation | ✅ PASS | IDs derived from `seed + type + index`; no `Math.random()` calls anywhere. |
| Domain Integrity | ✅ PASS | Square and rectangle are distinct types even though both map to `<rect>` in semantic mode. |
| Geometry First | ✅ PASS | No styling fields (opacity, fill, stroke, gradient) in Stage 1. |
| Library-First Core | ✅ PASS | Pure function, explicit I/O, no side effects. Independently testable. |
| Explicit Contracts | ✅ PASS | Canonicalization drops unknown fields; replay bundle = seed + canonical config. |
| Layer Boundaries | ✅ PASS | `src/` has no imports from application layers, composition, or rulebook. |

**Constitution Check Result**: All gates PASS. No violations to justify.

## Project Structure

### Documentation (this feature)

```text
specs/001-svg-shape-engine/
├── plan.md              # This file (/speckit.plan command output)
├── research.md          # Phase 0 output (/speckit.plan command)
├── data-model.md        # Phase 1 output (/speckit.plan command)
├── quickstart.md        # Phase 1 output (/speckit.plan command)
├── contracts/
│   └── api.md           # Phase 1 output (/speckit.plan command)
└── tasks.md             # Phase 2 output (/speckit.tasks command)
```

### Source Code (repository root)

```text
src/
├── index.ts             # Public entry point — exports generate()
├── types.ts             # All public and internal TypeScript types
├── generate.ts          # Orchestration: canonicalize → validate → render → assemble
├── canonicalize.ts      # Drops unknown fields, normalises input
├── validate.ts          # Rejects NaN/Infinity/missing required fields
├── id.ts                # Deterministic ShapeID generation
├── fmt.ts               # Numeric formatting (6 dp, trailing-zero trim)
├── svg.ts               # SVG root assembly
└── shapes/
    ├── square.ts
    ├── rectangle.ts
    ├── circle.ts
    └── triangle.ts

tests/
├── generate.test.ts     # End-to-end + determinism
├── canonicalize.test.ts # Canonicalization behaviour
├── validate.test.ts     # Error paths
├── id.test.ts           # ShapeID format
├── invariants.test.ts   # Cross-cutting invariants
└── shapes/
    ├── square.test.ts
    ├── rectangle.test.ts
    ├── circle.test.ts
    └── triangle.test.ts
```

**Structure Decision**: Single project. All source under `src/`; all tests under `tests/`. Mirrors the spec's "Project Structure" entries. No monorepo layout needed for a single-purpose library.
